channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: AIDS 2026 Young Leaders Programme 2026 for Young People Living With HIV, Activists and Researchers
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The International AIDS Society (IAS) has officially opened applications for the AIDS 2026 Young Leaders Programme, a prestigious international opportunity designed to empower young people living with HIV, youth activists, and emerging researchers in the global HIV response ecosystem. The programme is part of the 26th International AIDS Conference (AIDS 2026) and aims to strengthen […]

The post AIDS 2026 Young Leaders Programme 2026 for Young People Living With HIV, Activists and Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The International AIDS Society (IAS) has officially opened applications for the AIDS 2026 Young Leaders Programme, a prestigious international opportunity designed to empower young people living with HIV, youth activists, and emerging researchers in the global HIV response ecosystem.

The programme is part of the 26th International AIDS Conference (AIDS 2026) and aims to strengthen youth leadership, advocacy, networking, innovation, and career development within the HIV and public health sector.

Young leaders selected for the programme will gain direct exposure to global HIV experts, researchers, policymakers, advocates, and scientists while participating in a specially curated leadership and mentorship curriculum during the conference.

This initiative represents a major opportunity for young professionals and advocates passionate about HIV research, public health, social justice, activism, and global health leadership.

About the AIDS 2026 Young Leaders Programme

The Young Leaders Programme is an initiative offered at both the International AIDS Conference and the IAS Conference on HIV Science. It was created to support the next generation of leaders working in HIV advocacy, healthcare, research, and community mobilization.

The programme seeks to provide young participants with practical exposure, professional connections, mentorship, and opportunities to actively contribute to global conversations around HIV prevention, treatment, advocacy, policy, and innovation.

Selected Young Leaders will participate in exclusive sessions designed to build leadership capacity and foster collaboration among young change-makers from around the world.

The programme particularly supports:

  • Young people living with HIV
  • Young activists
  • Young researchers
  • Emerging public health leaders
  • Youth advocates working in HIV-related fields

What Participants Will Gain

Young Leaders selected for AIDS 2026 will benefit from an extensive learning and networking experience that goes beyond traditional conference participation.

Programme Benefits Include:

  1. Meetings With Global HIV Leaders
    Participants will engage directly with leading scientists, researchers, policymakers, advocates, and AIDS 2026 featured speakers.
  2. Innovation Workshops
    Young Leaders will participate in collaborative workshops designed to help develop innovative ideas and solutions related to HIV advocacy, healthcare delivery, prevention, and research.
  3. One-on-One Career Forum
    Participants will receive personalized career guidance and mentorship focused on professional pathways within the HIV and public health sector.
  4. International Networking Opportunities
    The programme provides access to a global network of:
  • Young activists
  • Researchers
  • Community advocates
  • Public health professionals
  • Youth leaders living with HIV
  1. Leadership Development
    Young Leaders will strengthen their:
  • Advocacy skills
  • Public engagement capacity
  • Research communication
  • Leadership competencies
  • Collaborative problem-solving skills

Who Can Apply?

To qualify for the AIDS 2026 Young Leaders Programme, applicants must meet the following eligibility requirements:

  • Must be aged 29 years or younger
  • Must be registered to attend AIDS 2026 in person
  • Must be interested or active in HIV advocacy, research, activism, or community engagement
  • Must apply through their conference profile

The programme welcomes applications from young individuals with diverse experiences and backgrounds.

Important Dates

Application Opening Date

4 May 2026

Application Deadline

18 May 2026

Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications early to avoid last-minute technical challenges.

How to Apply

Applications for the AIDS 2026 Young Leaders Programme must be submitted through the applicant’s official conference profile.

Application Process

  1. Register to attend AIDS 2026 in person
  2. Access your conference profile
  3. Complete the Young Leaders Programme application
  4. Submit before the deadline

Official Application Link

APPLY HERE

Why This Opportunity Matters

The HIV response movement has always been strengthened by youth leadership and community-driven innovation. The AIDS 2026 Young Leaders Programme provides a platform for young people to:

  • Influence global discussions
  • Build meaningful partnerships
  • Advance their careers
  • Share lived experiences
  • Contribute to solutions that improve health outcomes worldwide

For many participants, this programme can become a gateway to long-term involvement in international health leadership, research collaborations, and advocacy initiatives.

Frequently Asked Questions

Will Young Leaders Receive Funding?

Applicants are encouraged to consult the official FAQs for information regarding funding and support.

How Many Young Leaders Will Be Selected?

The IAS has not publicly specified the exact number of selected participants.

Where Can Applicants Ask Questions?

For additional inquiries:
YouthHub@iasociety.org

About the International AIDS Society (IAS)

The International AIDS Society is one of the world’s leading independent associations of HIV professionals. Through conferences, scientific engagement, advocacy, and community programmes, IAS works to accelerate the global HIV response and improve access to research, treatment, prevention, and care.

The AIDS Conference remains one of the largest global gatherings focused on HIV science, policy, activism, and public health collaboration.

Final Thoughts

The AIDS 2026 Young Leaders Programme is more than just a conference opportunity—it is a leadership platform for the next generation of HIV advocates, researchers, and changemakers.

Young people who are passionate about HIV advocacy, community impact, healthcare innovation, public health research, and global leadership should strongly consider applying for this transformative programme.

With access to international experts, networking opportunities, innovation workshops, and career mentorship, participants will gain valuable experience that can shape their future impact in the global health sector.

For more information on this fellowship visit here 

Discover more fellowship opportunities on OFY on here 

The post AIDS 2026 Young Leaders Programme 2026 for Young People Living With HIV, Activists and Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: IFRA-Nairobi Food Circulations in Africa Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026 | Fully Funded East Africa Research Opportunity for Social Scientists
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
Researchers and scholars interested in food systems, social sciences, and African studies now have an exciting opportunity to advance their academic careers through the IFRA-Nairobi Food Circulations in Africa Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026. The programme is designed to support innovative research on the social and spatial biographies of food objects across East Africa while contributing to […]

The post IFRA-Nairobi Food Circulations in Africa Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026 | Fully Funded East Africa Research Opportunity for Social Scientists appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Researchers and scholars interested in food systems, social sciences, and African studies now have an exciting opportunity to advance their academic careers through the IFRA-Nairobi Food Circulations in Africa Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026. The programme is designed to support innovative research on the social and spatial biographies of food objects across East Africa while contributing to a growing body of interdisciplinary scholarship on food circulation, mobility, culture, and society.

The Institut Français de Recherche en Afrique (IFRA-Nairobi) is offering postdoctoral fellowships ranging from three to six months for qualified researchers conducting studies connected to food circulation and food object biographies in East Africa. Selected fellows will receive fieldwork support valued at 800 Euros per month to facilitate their research activities.

This fellowship is part of the broader “Food Circulations in Africa” programme, which seeks to document the social life of food objects in Africa and develop methodologies for analysing how food moves through social, economic, political, and geographical spaces.

About the IFRA-Nairobi Food Circulations in Africa Programme

The “Food Circulations in Africa” programme focuses on understanding the movement, transformation, and social significance of food objects across African societies. Rather than examining food solely as nutrition or commerce, the programme explores food as a cultural, historical, political, and social object with evolving biographies shaped by human interactions, migration, trade, identity, and globalization.

The initiative aims to produce contextualized social and spatial biographies of food objects, helping scholars and policymakers better understand the interconnected dynamics of food systems in Africa.

Through this postdoctoral fellowship, IFRA-Nairobi supports researchers who can contribute fresh insights into:

  • Food circulation networks
  • Food histories and identities
  • Agricultural and trade systems
  • Migration and food mobility
  • Consumption practices
  • Political economies of food
  • Cultural meanings attached to food objects
  • Social transformation through food systems

Fellowship Duration and Financial Support

Successful applicants will receive:

  • A postdoctoral fellowship lasting between 3 and 6 months
  • A fieldwork grant of 800 Euros per month
  • Research support from IFRA-Nairobi
  • Opportunities to contribute to regional and international academic discussions on food studies and African research

The fellowship is specifically intended to support field-based research activities in East Africa.

Research Areas and Academic Focus

The programme welcomes interdisciplinary research projects connected to the social and spatial biographies of food objects in East Africa.

Applicants are encouraged to explore themes such as:

1. Food Mobility and Circulation

Research examining how food products move across local, national, and regional boundaries.

2. Food and Identity

Studies exploring cultural identity, heritage, and community relationships linked to food.

3. Political and Economic Dimensions of Food

Projects analysing food governance, trade systems, food security, and agricultural economies.

4. Historical and Anthropological Perspectives

Research documenting historical food pathways, traditions, and evolving food practices.

5. “Follow the Thing” Methodologies

Projects inspired by methodologies that trace the movement and transformation of objects through social systems.

Eligible Countries and Research Regions

The fellowship targets researchers conducting studies in East Africa, specifically in:

  • Kenya
  • Uganda
  • Tanzania
  • Rwanda
  • Burundi
  • Eastern Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC)

Applicants must currently reside in a country within the East African region.

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for the IFRA-Nairobi Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026, applicants must meet the following requirements:

Academic Qualifications

Applicants must:

  • Have defended a PhD thesis in the social sciences or humanities after January 1, 2020

Research Areas

Candidates should conduct research related to East Africa in one or more of the following disciplines:

  • Geography
  • Anthropology
  • Political Science
  • History
  • Related social science and humanities fields

Residency Requirement

Applicants must reside in an East African country.

Age Requirement

Applicants must be under 45 years old.

Required Application Documents

Applicants are expected to prepare and submit the following materials:

1. Cover Letter

The cover letter should:

  • Be approximately 2 pages long
  • Include a 15–20 line summary of the research project
  • Explain the applicant’s profile and research interests
  • Highlight experience in food studies or related research fields
  • Include contact information for Master’s or PhD supervisors
  • Mention any additional institutional support or funding received

The document must be submitted in PDF format.

2. Detailed Curriculum Vitae (CV)

The CV should include:

  • Academic background
  • Research experience
  • Relevant publications
  • Professional achievements
  • Conferences and academic contributions

The CV must be submitted in PDF format.

3. Research Proposal

The proposal should be a maximum of 3 pages and must contain:

  • Research context
  • Research questions
  • Methodology
  • Bibliography
  • Fieldwork location details
  • Research timeline and planning

Applicants are required to submit the proposal in both PDF and Word formats.

Application Deadline

The deadline for submitting applications is:

26 June 2026

Late submissions may not be considered.

How to Apply

Applications should be submitted via email to:

📧 info@ifra-nairobi.net

Additional Information:
IFRA-Nairobi Fellowship Information

Why This Fellowship Matters

The IFRA-Nairobi Food Circulations in Africa Fellowship represents an important opportunity for emerging scholars interested in food systems, African studies, and interdisciplinary social science research.

The programme provides:

  • Financial support for fieldwork
  • Academic visibility
  • Regional research engagement
  • Opportunities for interdisciplinary collaboration
  • Access to networks in African food studies and humanities research

As food systems continue to shape debates around sustainability, culture, mobility, and economic development, this fellowship supports research that contributes to understanding Africa’s rapidly changing food landscapes.

Discover more fellowship opportunities on OFY on here 

The post IFRA-Nairobi Food Circulations in Africa Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026 | Fully Funded East Africa Research Opportunity for Social Scientists appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Global Artivism 2026 Remote Opportunities: Paid Global Roles for Artists, Activists, Cultural Organizers, and Movement Builders Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
Global Artivism has announced multiple remote global opportunities for 2026, offering paid roles for cultural organizers, activists, researchers, campaign coordinators, and operational professionals across different world regions. These opportunities are designed for individuals deeply committed to social justice, cultural transformation, ecological integrity, and collective liberation. Led by Global Majority communities, Global Artivism is building a […]

The post Global Artivism 2026 Remote Opportunities: Paid Global Roles for Artists, Activists, Cultural Organizers, and Movement Builders Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Global Artivism has announced multiple remote global opportunities for 2026, offering paid roles for cultural organizers, activists, researchers, campaign coordinators, and operational professionals across different world regions. These opportunities are designed for individuals deeply committed to social justice, cultural transformation, ecological integrity, and collective liberation.

Led by Global Majority communities, Global Artivism is building a worldwide movement that harnesses the power of arts and culture to drive systemic change. The organization is seeking talented professionals and community-rooted changemakers to support major initiatives such as Global Artivism Month, regional ecosystem mapping, movement coordination, and global cultural activism campaigns.

The available opportunities include:

  1. Admin Coordinator
  2. Global Artivism Month Specialist
  3. Regional Fellow (Africa)
  4. Regional Fellow (Americas & Caribbean)
  5. Regional Fellow (Asia)
  6. Regional Fellow (Europe)
  7. Regional Fellow (Middle East / West Asia)
  8. Regional Fellow (Oceania / Pacific)

These positions are fully remote and open globally or regionally depending on the role. Most opportunities prioritize candidates from the Global South and Global Majority communities.

About Global Artivism

Global Artivism is a Global Majority-led movement that uses arts and culture as powerful tools for justice, liberation, and systems change.

The movement believes that art is not separate from activism but is instead an active force that can:

  • Shift narratives
  • Mobilize communities
  • Influence public consciousness
  • Build solidarity
  • Reshape systems of power

Global Artivism connects artists, activists, cultural workers, organizers, and movement builders across multiple regions of the world. The movement is governed by a Global Council representing diverse regions and communities globally.

Core Principles of Global Artivism

The organization’s work is guided by several foundational principles:

  1. Global Majority Leadership and South–South Solidarity
  2. Distributed Power and Fluid Leadership
  3. Artists at the Center, Culture as Strategy
  4. Intersectional and Community-Centered Organizing
  5. Trust Before Scale
  6. Emergence as Method
  7. Transparency and Accountability

These principles shape every aspect of the organization’s programming, campaigns, collaborations, and operational structure.

1. Admin Coordinator Opportunity 2026

Global Artivism is hiring an experienced administrative professional to support the Movement Secretariat through coordination, scheduling, logistics, and communication management.

Contract Details

  • Compensation: USD $1,500–$2,500/month
  • Location: Remote (Global)
  • Priority: Candidates from the Global South
  • Duration: June 1, 2026 – May 31, 2027
  • Type: Independent Contractor

Key Responsibilities

The Admin Coordinator will:

  • Coordinate meetings across multiple time zones
  • Support virtual and in-person event logistics
  • Draft correspondence and manage documentation
  • Organize schedules and action items
  • Maintain systems across Google Workspace, Slack, Zoom, and project management tools
  • Coordinate travel arrangements and meeting logistics

Qualifications

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Strong organizational and administrative experience
  • Ability to support distributed international teams
  • Excellent communication skills in English
  • Experience with digital collaboration platforms
  • High discretion and professionalism
  • Alignment with Global Artivism values

Community organizing and grassroots movement support experience are valued equally alongside formal administrative employment.

Learn More Here

2. Global Artivism Month Specialist 2026

This role focuses on leading the delivery of Global Artivism Month, a major flagship programme planned for October/November 2026.

Contract Details

  • Compensation: USD $3,500–$5,000/month
  • Location: Remote (Global)
  • Duration: June 1 – December 31, 2026
  • Type: Independent Contractor

Key Responsibilities

The specialist will:

  1. Coordinate Global Artivism Month campaign delivery
  2. Build campaign toolkits and partner resources
  3. Manage timelines, budgets, and logistics
  4. Mobilize regional partners and community networks
  5. Facilitate steering committee engagement
  6. Lead post-campaign analysis and reporting

Desired Experience

Global Artivism is seeking candidates with:

  • Strong programme management skills
  • Campaign coordination experience
  • Experience in social justice, arts, or cultural activism
  • Community mobilization expertise
  • Multilingual ability (preferred)
  • Experience coordinating across global teams

The organization strongly values applicants with Global South and movement-building experience.

Learn More Here

3. Regional Fellowship Opportunities 2026

Global Artivism is also recruiting six Regional Fellows to strengthen regional Artivist ecosystems and coordinate community activation initiatives.

Available Regions

Regional Fellow positions are available for:

  • Africa
  • Americas & Caribbean
  • Asia
  • Europe
  • Middle East / West Asia
  • Oceania / Pacific

Fellowship Details

  • Compensation: USD $900–$1,500/month
  • Duration: June 1 – December 31, 2026
  • Type: Independent Contractor
  • Work Format: Fully Remote

Purpose of the Fellowship

Regional Fellows will help:

  • Build regional Artivist ecosystems
  • Conduct cultural activism mapping and research
  • Organize regional gatherings and exchanges
  • Support Global Artivism Month planning
  • Build solidarity across regional movements

Main Responsibilities

Regional Fellows will:

  1. Map artists, activists, organizations, and cultural movements
  2. Produce ecosystem reports and research outputs
  3. Convene regional gatherings and peer exchanges
  4. Support campaign coordination and storytelling
  5. Participate in cross-regional learning exchanges

Qualifications

Applicants should have:

  • Strong community grounding in their region
  • Experience in activism, organizing, or cultural work
  • Research and facilitation skills
  • Ability to coordinate networks and gatherings
  • Strong written English communication
  • Familiarity with social justice and movement ecosystems

Global Artivism particularly values lived experience, grassroots work, and community leadership.

Compensation Across Opportunities

The programme offers competitive contractor fees depending on the role:

Role Compensation
Admin Coordinator USD $1,500–$2,500/month
Global Artivism Month Specialist USD $3,500–$5,000/month
Regional Fellows USD $900–$1,500/month

All positions are independent contractor engagements. Contractors are responsible for their own taxes, registrations, and compliance requirements within their countries.

What Global Artivism Offers

Selected participants will receive:

  • Access to a global network of Artivists and changemakers
  • Professional development opportunities
  • Participation in international movement-building spaces
  • Flexible remote work arrangements
  • Cross-regional collaboration opportunities
  • Exposure to global arts and justice ecosystems

Regional Fellows will additionally join a cohort of fellows from six global regions.

How to Apply

Applicants must send their applications via email to:

team@globalartivism.ca

Application Deadline

May 17, 2026 at midnight SAST (South Africa Standard Time).

Required Documents

Applicants must submit:

  1. A CV (maximum two pages)
  2. A short written response (300–400 words) addressing role-specific questions
  3. One reference with contact information

Shortlisted candidates will be contacted by May 25, 2026 and invited to interview rounds.

Why These Opportunities Matter

Global Artivism’s 2026 opportunities are more than jobs or fellowships. They are invitations to participate in a growing global movement that recognizes culture and creativity as central tools for justice and transformation.

These opportunities are particularly valuable for:

  • Artists working in activism spaces
  • Community organizers
  • Cultural strategists
  • Social justice advocates
  • Movement builders
  • Researchers focused on arts and social change

The organization’s emphasis on Global Majority leadership and community-rooted experience makes these opportunities especially inclusive for underrepresented voices and grassroots leaders globally.

Discover more Job, Fellowship, and Leadership Development opportunities on OFY.

The post Global Artivism 2026 Remote Opportunities: Paid Global Roles for Artists, Activists, Cultural Organizers, and Movement Builders Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Foresight Fellowship 2027: Fully Supported Global Fellowship for Early-Career Scientists, Engineers, and Technology Innovators
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The Foresight Institute has officially opened applications for the highly prestigious Foresight Fellowship 2027, a year-long international fellowship designed to support exceptional early-career scientists, engineers, researchers, entrepreneurs, and innovators working on transformative technologies. The fellowship provides selected participants with access to world-class mentorship, career development support, global research networks, technical workshops, funding connections, and exclusive […]

The post Foresight Fellowship 2027: Fully Supported Global Fellowship for Early-Career Scientists, Engineers, and Technology Innovators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Foresight Institute has officially opened applications for the highly prestigious Foresight Fellowship 2027, a year-long international fellowship designed to support exceptional early-career scientists, engineers, researchers, entrepreneurs, and innovators working on transformative technologies.

The fellowship provides selected participants with access to world-class mentorship, career development support, global research networks, technical workshops, funding connections, and exclusive opportunities to collaborate with some of the leading thinkers shaping the future of science and technology.

Focused on accelerating breakthrough innovation, the Foresight Fellowship is ideal for ambitious individuals seeking to make meaningful contributions in areas such as artificial intelligence, neurotechnology, biotechnology, nanotechnology, space innovation, and existential risk reduction.

About the Foresight Fellowship

The Foresight Fellowship is a globally recognized program aimed at empowering visionary early-career innovators by connecting them with the networks, mentorship, and resources necessary to advance frontier technologies.

The program is built on the belief that talented individuals can make transformative contributions when given early access to:

  • Senior scientific mentorship
  • Collaborative research communities
  • Funding networks
  • Knowledge exchange platforms
  • High-impact scientific ecosystems

The fellowship supports participants in developing both their technical expertise and long-term career trajectories.

What the Fellowship Offers

Selected Fellows receive extensive professional and research support throughout the year-long program.

Key Benefits of the Fellowship

1. Access to Senior Scientists and Mentors

Fellows are introduced to:

  • Leading researchers
  • Industry experts
  • Experienced mentors
  • Influential funders
  • Frontier technology innovators

These connections help Fellows strengthen their work and accelerate career growth.

2. Paid Participation in Major Scientific Events

The fellowship includes:

  • Attendance at technical workshops
  • Participation in the Vision Weekend conference
  • Fully funded travel support

These events gather influential leaders working at the cutting edge of science and technology.

3. Membership in Specialized Seminar Groups

Fellows gain access to:

  • Research seminars
  • Knowledge-sharing communities
  • Opportunities to present their work
  • Collaborative learning environments

This allows participants to receive feedback and engage with experts in their field.

4. Priority Access to AI Research Hubs

Fellows receive priority access to Foresight Institute AI Nodes located in:

  • San Francisco
  • Berlin

These hubs support researchers and innovators working on AI for science and AI safety.

5. Career Counseling and Long-Term Community Support

The fellowship also offers:

  • Quarterly career counseling calls
  • Guidance from industry leaders
  • Long-term alumni network access
  • Continued collaboration opportunities

Participants become part of the broader Foresight Fellowship Alumni community after completing the program.

Focus Areas of the Fellowship

The fellowship supports projects and innovators working across several frontier science and technology domains.

1. Secure AI

Areas include:

  • Computer security
  • AI alignment
  • Secure multiagent systems
  • AI safety research

2. Longevity Biotechnology

Focus areas include:

  • Research automation
  • Healthcare innovation
  • Reversible biostasis
  • Longevity science

3. Nanotechnology

Applicants may work on:

  • Molecular 3D printing
  • Molecular simulation
  • Advanced nanomaterials
  • Nanotechnology modeling systems

4. Neurotechnology

Projects may involve:

  • Brain-computer interfaces
  • Whole brain emulation
  • Neuro-inspired AI systems
  • Cognitive enhancement technologies

5. Existential Hope

The fellowship also supports work in:

  • Technological alignment
  • Strategic foresight
  • Existential opportunity
  • Future worldbuilding
  • Global resilience

6. Space Innovation

The fellowship welcomes innovators developing technologies and systems related to space exploration and sustainability.

Who the Fellowship Is Looking For

The Foresight Fellowship seeks highly motivated individuals with strong technical capabilities and ambitious visions for the future.

Ideal Candidates Should Demonstrate

1. Technical Excellence

Applicants should have:

  • Strong academic or professional achievements
  • Research publications
  • Innovative projects
  • Laboratory experience
  • Proven technical expertise

2. Ambitious Vision

The fellowship prioritizes “moonshot” thinkers who:

  • Challenge conventional thinking
  • Pursue transformative ideas
  • Aim to solve major global problems
  • Develop futuristic solutions

3. Commitment to Growth

Successful candidates are expected to demonstrate:

  • Intellectual curiosity
  • Continuous learning
  • Passion for innovation
  • Desire to collaborate and expand their capabilities

4. Potential for Societal Impact

The program strongly values projects capable of generating meaningful positive impact on society and humanity’s future.

Why This Fellowship Matters

The Foresight Fellowship stands out because it combines:

  • Mentorship
  • Scientific collaboration
  • Career development
  • Networking
  • Frontier research exposure

Unlike traditional fellowships, the program offers a highly flexible environment with no minimum work requirement, focusing instead on empowering exceptional individuals to maximize their potential.

The fellowship creates opportunities for participants to accelerate groundbreaking work capable of shaping the future of science and technology globally.

Networking and Community Opportunities

Participants become part of an international community of:

  • Scientists
  • Engineers
  • Entrepreneurs
  • AI researchers
  • Biotech innovators
  • Futurists
  • Technology builders

This collaborative ecosystem helps Fellows exchange ideas, develop partnerships, and gain visibility for their work.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can apply for the 2027 cohort through the official Foresight Fellowship application portal.

Official Website:
Foresight Fellowship Official Website

Applicants are encouraged to prepare detailed information showcasing:

  • Their technical work
  • Research achievements
  • Future vision
  • Potential societal impact
  • Innovative projects

Apply HERE

Final Thoughts

The Foresight Fellowship 2027 presents an exceptional opportunity for early-career innovators seeking to work at the forefront of transformative science and technology.

By offering mentorship, research exposure, global networking opportunities, funded event participation, and long-term career support, the fellowship empowers visionary individuals to accelerate breakthrough innovations that could shape humanity’s future.

Scientists, engineers, entrepreneurs, and researchers with bold ideas and strong technical foundations should strongly consider applying for this prestigious global fellowship.

For more information about the opportunity, visit the official website

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT

The post Foresight Fellowship 2027: Fully Supported Global Fellowship for Early-Career Scientists, Engineers, and Technology Innovators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNU-WIDER Internship Programme 2026 in Finland: Paid International Internship Opportunity with the United Nations University for Students and Recent Graduates
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The United Nations University World Institute for Development Economics Research has officially opened applications for the prestigious UNU-WIDER Internship Programme 2026, offering students and recent graduates the opportunity to gain hands-on professional experience within the United Nations system while contributing to global sustainable development efforts. The internship programme provides participants with practical exposure to international […]

The post UNU-WIDER Internship Programme 2026 in Finland: Paid International Internship Opportunity with the United Nations University for Students and Recent Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations University World Institute for Development Economics Research has officially opened applications for the prestigious UNU-WIDER Internship Programme 2026, offering students and recent graduates the opportunity to gain hands-on professional experience within the United Nations system while contributing to global sustainable development efforts.

The internship programme provides participants with practical exposure to international development, economic research, partnerships, programme support, and operational innovation in one of the world’s leading development economics research institutions.

Hosted in Helsinki, Finland, the programme is designed for highly motivated individuals interested in supporting work aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) while gaining experience in a dynamic, multicultural, and internationally respected workplace.

The programme offers multiple internship opportunities across different units within UNU-WIDER, allowing selected interns to contribute directly to research, partnerships, and operational projects.

About UNU-WIDER

The United Nations University World Institute for Development Economics Research (UNU-WIDER) is part of the broader United Nations University system and serves as a leading international research institution focused on:

  • Economic development
  • Poverty reduction
  • Inequality
  • Sustainable development
  • Public policy research
  • Global economic transformation

Based in Helsinki, Finland, UNU-WIDER produces evidence-based research and policy analysis aimed at supporting inclusive and sustainable development worldwide.

The institute collaborates with:

  • Governments
  • International organizations
  • Academic institutions
  • Development agencies
  • Researchers and policymakers globally

About the UNU-WIDER Internship Programme

The UNU-WIDER Internship Programme is designed to:

  • Provide practical professional experience
  • Strengthen participants’ understanding of UN operations
  • Develop technical and analytical skills
  • Support career development in international development and policy
  • Expose participants to development economics research and global policy work

Interns become part of an international working environment where they contribute to ongoing institutional activities and projects.

Available Internship Positions for 2026

UNU-WIDER is currently accepting applications for the following internship opportunities.

1. Microsoft Power Platform Solutions Intern

Unit:

Operational Support Unit

Internship Period:

15 August 2026 – 14 February 2027

Main Focus Areas:

  • Microsoft Power Platform solutions
  • Operational process support
  • Digital tools and automation
  • Organizational systems improvement

This role is ideal for applicants interested in:

  • Technology
  • Digital transformation
  • Process automation
  • Data systems
  • Operational innovation

Learn More & Apply Here

2. Programme and Partnerships Intern

Unit:

Partnerships and Programme Support Unit

Internship Period:

1 September 2026 – 28 February 2027

Main Focus Areas:

  • Programme coordination
  • Institutional partnerships
  • Stakeholder engagement
  • Administrative support
  • Development cooperation

This internship is suitable for candidates interested in:

  • International development
  • Partnerships management
  • Project coordination
  • Policy engagement
  • Programme administration

Learn More & Apply Here

3. Research Intern

Unit:

Research Unit

Internship Period:

1 September 2026 – 28 February 2027

Main Focus Areas:

  • Development economics research
  • Policy analysis
  • Research support
  • Data analysis
  • Academic collaboration

This position is particularly relevant for applicants pursuing careers in:

  • Economics
  • Public policy
  • Research
  • Academia
  • International development

Learn More & Apply Here

About the United Nations University

The United Nations University (UNU) is the academic and research arm of the United Nations, focused on:

  • Knowledge generation
  • Policy research
  • Capacity building
  • Sustainable development solutions

UNU-WIDER plays a major role in advancing research and evidence-based policymaking related to global development challenges.

Benefits of the UNU-WIDER Internship Programme

The internship programme offers participants a valuable opportunity to build practical international experience while working within the United Nations system.

1. Hands-On UN Experience

Interns gain direct exposure to:

  • UN institutional operations
  • International development work
  • Global policy processes
  • Research and programme implementation

2. Professional Development

Participants strengthen:

  • Analytical skills
  • Research capabilities
  • Communication skills
  • Technical competencies
  • International work experience

3. International Networking

Interns work alongside:

  • Researchers
  • Development professionals
  • Policy experts
  • International colleagues
  • Global stakeholders

4. Exposure to Sustainable Development Work

Participants contribute to initiatives connected to:

  • Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)
  • Economic development
  • Poverty reduction
  • Inclusive growth
  • Global policy solutions

5. Career Advancement Opportunities

Experience within the United Nations system can strengthen future opportunities in:

  • International organizations
  • Research institutions
  • Development agencies
  • NGOs
  • Government policy institutions

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for the UNU-WIDER Internship Programme, applicants must meet the following criteria.

Academic Eligibility

Applicants must be:

  • In the final year of undergraduate study in a relevant field, OR
  • Enrolled in a graduate degree programme, OR
  • Have graduated within the last two years

Interest in UN Work

Candidates should demonstrate:

  • Strong interest in international development
  • Motivation to contribute to the Sustainable Development Goals
  • Interest in economics, policy, or global development work

Availability Requirement

Applicants must be able to:

  • Commit to a six-month internship period
  • Start in August or September 2026 depending on the role

Residence and Work Permit Requirement

Applicants must possess:

  • A valid residence permit in Finland
  • A valid work permit in Finland

This is a mandatory eligibility requirement for all positions.

Why This Internship Opportunity Matters

The UNU-WIDER Internship Programme stands out because it combines:

  • Practical UN experience
  • Academic and policy exposure
  • Research opportunities
  • Professional development
  • International collaboration

Unlike many internships focused solely on administrative tasks, this programme offers interns the opportunity to contribute meaningfully to real-world development research and institutional activities.

Skills Applicants Can Gain

Participants may develop skills in:

  • Research and data analysis
  • International development policy
  • Programme management
  • Stakeholder engagement
  • Technical systems support
  • Professional communication
  • Cross-cultural collaboration

Ideal Candidates

This programme is especially suitable for:

  • Students interested in UN careers
  • Aspiring development economists
  • Policy researchers
  • International affairs students
  • Public administration students
  • Technology and systems innovation students
  • Development practitioners

Application Deadline

The official application deadline for the UNU-WIDER Internship Programme 2026 is:

24 May 2026

Applicants are encouraged to submit applications early and carefully review the requirements for their preferred internship position.

Application Process

Interested applicants should:

  1. Select their preferred internship role
  2. Review the detailed position requirements
  3. Prepare application materials
  4. Submit applications through the official UNU-WIDER application portal

Contact Information

For further information or support regarding the internship programme, applicants may contact:

wider.internship@wider.unu.edu

Discover more global Internship and UN opportunities on OFY.

The post UNU-WIDER Internship Programme 2026 in Finland: Paid International Internship Opportunity with the United Nations University for Students and Recent Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Zayed Sustainability Prize 2027: Global Call for Innovative Sustainability Solutions with US$7.2 Million Prize Fund
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The prestigious Zayed Sustainability Prize has officially opened submissions for its 2027 awards cycle, inviting innovators, nonprofit organisations, enterprises, and schools from around the world to submit transformative sustainability solutions that can improve lives and address urgent global challenges. Now in its 18th year, the Zayed Sustainability Prize has become one of the world’s most […]

The post Zayed Sustainability Prize 2027: Global Call for Innovative Sustainability Solutions with US$7.2 Million Prize Fund appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The prestigious Zayed Sustainability Prize has officially opened submissions for its 2027 awards cycle, inviting innovators, nonprofit organisations, enterprises, and schools from around the world to submit transformative sustainability solutions that can improve lives and address urgent global challenges.

Now in its 18th year, the Zayed Sustainability Prize has become one of the world’s most influential sustainability awards, recognizing groundbreaking initiatives that drive positive social, environmental, and economic impact.

With an increased total prize fund of US$7.2 million, the 2027 cycle presents a major opportunity for changemakers working in sustainability sectors to scale their impact and gain global recognition.

About the Zayed Sustainability Prize

Established in 2008, the Zayed Sustainability Prize was created to honor the legacy and vision of the late Sheikh Zayed bin Sultan Al Nahyan, whose commitment to sustainability and humanitarian development continues to inspire global action.

The Prize supports organizations and schools that develop innovative and scalable solutions capable of addressing some of the world’s most pressing sustainability challenges.

Since its inception, the Prize has empowered global innovators whose work has significantly improved lives across communities worldwide.

Prize Categories

The Zayed Sustainability Prize recognizes excellence across several critical sustainability sectors.

Categories Include

1. Health

Solutions improving healthcare access, affordability, and health outcomes.

2. Food

Innovations promoting food security, sustainable agriculture, and nutrition.

3. Energy

Projects advancing clean energy access and renewable energy solutions.

4. Water

Initiatives addressing water scarcity, sanitation, and safe drinking water access.

5. Climate Action

Solutions focused on climate resilience, environmental protection, and carbon reduction.

6. Global High Schools

Student-led sustainability projects implemented within educational institutions.

Why the Zayed Sustainability Prize Matters

The Prize is globally respected because it not only recognizes innovation but also helps amplify impact.

The initiative supports projects capable of:

  • Improving livelihoods
  • Expanding access to essential services
  • Protecting the environment
  • Building climate resilience
  • Supporting sustainable development goals

It particularly emphasizes scalable solutions that can create measurable long-term impact across communities.

Global Impact of the Prize

Through its network of winners and finalists, the Zayed Sustainability Prize has already achieved remarkable global outcomes.

Impact Achievements Include

  • Over 400 million lives transformed
  • More than 1.39 billion tonnes of CO₂ emissions reduced
  • Over 11.4 million people gaining access to safe drinking water
  • More than 17 million people gaining access to nutritious food
  • Approximately 841,300 jobs created
  • More than 1.2 million people gaining access to affordable healthcare
  • Over 480,660 community members benefiting from high school sustainability projects

These achievements demonstrate the Prize’s role in accelerating sustainable innovation worldwide.

Who Should Apply?

The Prize welcomes applications from:

  • Startups
  • Small and medium enterprises (SMEs)
  • Nonprofit organisations
  • Social enterprises
  • Educational institutions and high schools

Applicants are encouraged to submit projects that are:

  • Innovative
  • Scalable
  • Impact-driven
  • Sustainable
  • Community-centered

What Makes a Strong Application?

Successful submissions typically demonstrate:

  1. Clear sustainability impact
  2. Measurable social or environmental outcomes
  3. Innovation and originality
  4. Scalability and long-term viability
  5. Potential for global or regional replication

The Prize particularly values solutions that address urgent global challenges while creating lasting benefits for communities.

Awards Cycle Timeline

The 2027 cycle follows a structured timeline:

                    Stage Timeline
Submissions Open January 2026
Submissions  Close June 2026
Shortlisting of Applications August 2026
Finalists Announced September 2026
Awards Ceremony January 2027

Benefits of Winning the Prize

Winning or becoming a finalist in the Zayed Sustainability Prize offers far more than financial support.

Key Benefits Include

1. Global Recognition

Winners gain international visibility and credibility within the sustainability sector.

2. Financial Support

The Prize provides significant funding to help scale impactful solutions.

3. Networking Opportunities

Participants connect with:

  • Global sustainability leaders
  • Policymakers
  • Investors
  • Development organizations
  • Innovation ecosystems

4. Expanded Impact

Winning projects often experience accelerated growth, enabling broader implementation and outreach.

Focus on Sustainability Innovation

The Prize aligns strongly with global sustainability priorities, including:

  • Climate action
  • Renewable energy access
  • Food security
  • Water sustainability
  • Healthcare innovation
  • Community resilience

By supporting innovative changemakers, the initiative contributes to advancing global sustainable development efforts.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should prepare detailed submissions highlighting:

  • The problem being addressed
  • The innovation or solution
  • Demonstrated impact
  • Scalability potential
  • Sustainability strategy

Application Deadline

Submissions for the 2027 cycle close in:  22 June 2026

Apply Here

Final Thoughts

The Zayed Sustainability Prize 2027 stands as one of the most influential global sustainability awards available today.

With millions of dollars in funding, worldwide recognition, and a strong track record of supporting impactful solutions, the Prize provides an extraordinary platform for innovators committed to improving lives and protecting the planet.

Organizations, enterprises, nonprofits, and schools working on transformative sustainability initiatives should strongly consider applying before the June 2026 submission deadline.

For more information visit the official website

Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY, VISIT

The post Zayed Sustainability Prize 2027: Global Call for Innovative Sustainability Solutions with US$7.2 Million Prize Fund appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Global Youth Empowerment Fund (YEF) 2026: Apply for Up to €5,000 in Funding for Youth-Led Community Impact Projects Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
Applications are now officially open for the 2026 Global Youth Empowerment Fund (YEF), an international micro-grant initiative designed to support young changemakers and youth-led initiatives creating meaningful social impact in communities around the world. The Youth Empowerment Fund (YEF) provides young people with access to non-repayable funding, global visibility, and support networks to help transform […]

The post Global Youth Empowerment Fund (YEF) 2026: Apply for Up to €5,000 in Funding for Youth-Led Community Impact Projects Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now officially open for the 2026 Global Youth Empowerment Fund (YEF), an international micro-grant initiative designed to support young changemakers and youth-led initiatives creating meaningful social impact in communities around the world.

The Youth Empowerment Fund (YEF) provides young people with access to non-repayable funding, global visibility, and support networks to help transform innovative community ideas into practical, sustainable solutions.

The initiative is part of the broader Global Youth Mobilization movement, a global platform led by major youth organizations in partnership with international institutions focused on empowering youth action and leadership.

Through this funding opportunity, young leaders can access up to €5,000 in grant funding to implement projects addressing pressing social, environmental, educational, and developmental challenges.

About the Global Youth Empowerment Fund (YEF)

The Global Youth Empowerment Fund is a youth-focused funding initiative aimed at:

  • Supporting youth-led community solutions
  • Expanding access to funding for young innovators
  • Encouraging grassroots social impact
  • Promoting youth participation in sustainable development
  • Strengthening local and global youth leadership

The fund recognizes that many young people have innovative ideas and practical solutions for challenges within their communities but often lack access to the financial support necessary to implement them.

The programme helps bridge that gap by providing:

  • Micro-grant funding
  • Visibility opportunities
  • Global youth networking
  • Capacity-building support
  • Community impact resources

About Global Youth Mobilization

The Youth Empowerment Fund is part of the Global Youth Mobilization initiative, a global movement led by youth organizations and international partners focused on:

  • Youth empowerment
  • Community transformation
  • Sustainable development
  • Social innovation
  • Global youth participation

The initiative supports youth-driven solutions to some of the world’s most pressing challenges.

What the Fund Supports

The Youth Empowerment Fund supports projects focused on solving urgent local and global challenges in areas such as:

1. Community Development

Projects that:

  • Improve local communities
  • Promote inclusion and empowerment
  • Address social challenges
  • Support vulnerable populations

2. Climate Action

Initiatives focused on:

  • Environmental sustainability
  • Climate awareness
  • Green innovation
  • Community resilience
  • Environmental advocacy

3. Education and Skills Development

Projects that:

  • Expand learning opportunities
  • Improve youth education
  • Build employability skills
  • Promote digital literacy
  • Support vocational development

4. Health and Wellbeing

Initiatives supporting:

  • Mental health awareness
  • Public health education
  • Community wellbeing
  • Youth health interventions

5. Social Inclusion and Equality

Projects promoting:

  • Gender equality
  • Youth inclusion
  • Diversity and representation
  • Human rights and equity

Why the Youth Empowerment Fund Matters

Young people across the world are increasingly leading innovative social and environmental solutions, yet many face:

  • Limited access to funding
  • Lack of mentorship opportunities
  • Insufficient visibility
  • Resource barriers
  • Challenges scaling their ideas

The YEF seeks to remove these barriers by empowering youth-led initiatives with practical support and funding.

The programme places young people at the center of community transformation and recognizes youth as critical drivers of sustainable change.

Grant Value

Successful applicants may receive:

Up to €5,000 in Non-Repayable Funding

The micro-grant funding is intended to support the design, implementation, and growth of youth-led community impact projects.

Benefits of the Programme

Selected participants and organizations will gain access to several valuable opportunities.

1. Financial Support

Participants receive funding to implement and expand their projects.

2. Global Visibility

Projects gain increased international exposure and recognition.

3. Access to a Global Youth Network

Participants become connected to an international ecosystem of youth leaders and changemakers.

4. Capacity-Building Opportunities

Applicants may gain access to:

  • Training resources
  • Support materials
  • Knowledge-sharing opportunities
  • Community engagement support

5. Social Impact Development

The programme helps youth-led projects:

  • Scale their impact
  • Strengthen sustainability
  • Improve community engagement

Who Can Apply?

The Youth Empowerment Fund is open to:

  • Individuals
  • Youth groups
  • Youth-led organizations
  • Informal youth initiatives
  • Young social innovators

Applicants must:

  • Be between 14–30 years old
  • Lead youth-centered or community-focused initiatives
  • Demonstrate commitment to social impact
  • Present innovative project ideas

Eligible Project Types

The fund welcomes a broad range of youth-led initiatives, including:

  • Climate action campaigns
  • Education projects
  • Health awareness programmes
  • Social inclusion initiatives
  • Community development interventions
  • Youth empowerment activities
  • Sustainability innovations
  • Digital inclusion projects

Why This Opportunity Is Important

The Youth Empowerment Fund is more than a financial grant programme. It represents:

  • Recognition of youth leadership
  • Investment in grassroots innovation
  • Support for local problem-solving
  • Expansion of youth participation in development

The programme encourages young people to move from ideas to action by providing the support needed to create measurable impact.

Application Deadline

Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their applications before:

22nd May, 2026

Given the high global interest expected for this opportunity, early applications are highly recommended.

Application Process

Interested applicants should:

  1. Visit the official application portal
  2. Prepare details of their project idea
  3. Explain their community impact strategy
  4. Provide information about their team or organization
  5. Submit the completed application before the deadline

Click HERE to submit an application

Tips for a Strong Application

Applicants can improve their chances by:

  • Clearly explaining the community problem they want to solve
  • Demonstrating measurable impact potential
  • Showing strong youth leadership involvement
  • Presenting realistic implementation plans
  • Highlighting sustainability and long-term value
  • Emphasizing innovation and creativity

Click HERE to learn more about the Global Youth Empowerment Fund (YEF) 2026.

Discover more global Grant opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Global Youth Empowerment Fund (YEF) 2026: Apply for Up to €5,000 in Funding for Youth-Led Community Impact Projects Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Amplifying Young Voices for a Sustainable Future: FAO & AUN-AFS Research Proposal Competition for Youth 2026 Opens Applications
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO), in collaboration with the ASEAN University Network for Agriculture and Food Security (AUN-AFS), has officially launched the FAO & AUN-AFS Research Proposal Competition for Youth 2026, a transformative regional initiative designed to empower young researchers and master’s students to contribute innovative solutions toward building sustainable […]

The post Amplifying Young Voices for a Sustainable Future: FAO & AUN-AFS Research Proposal Competition for Youth 2026 Opens Applications appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO), in collaboration with the ASEAN University Network for Agriculture and Food Security (AUN-AFS), has officially launched the FAO & AUN-AFS Research Proposal Competition for Youth 2026, a transformative regional initiative designed to empower young researchers and master’s students to contribute innovative solutions toward building sustainable and resilient agrifood systems.

Announced during a landmark webinar held on 23 April 2026, this competition aims to bridge the gap between academic research and practical policy implementation by encouraging youth-led ideas, scientific innovation, and evidence-based solutions that address pressing food security and agricultural challenges in the Asia-Pacific region.

The initiative is open to eligible master’s students and young researchers from ASEAN countries, China, Japan, and South Korea, providing a unique opportunity for participants to showcase their research capabilities while receiving financial and academic support to advance their projects.

Why the FAO & AUN-AFS Research Proposal Competition Matters

Agrifood systems across the world are currently facing multiple challenges, including climate change, food insecurity, environmental degradation, and socioeconomic inequalities. Young people are increasingly recognized as key actors in creating sustainable solutions that can transform agriculture and food systems for future generations.

Through this competition, FAO and AUN-AFS aim to:

  • Encourage youth participation in agrifood systems transformation
  • Promote innovative research that aligns with FAO regional priorities
  • Support evidence-based policymaking
  • Foster collaboration between universities, researchers, and international organizations
  • Strengthen food security and sustainability across the region
  • Build leadership skills among young scholars and researchers

The competition represents a strategic investment in the next generation of researchers, innovators, and policymakers who will contribute to a healthier and more resilient global food system.

Financial and Academic Support for Selected Projects

One of the major highlights of the competition is that selected research proposals will receive financial support to help participants maximize the impact and quality of their research projects.

This support is intended to help young researchers develop practical, scalable, and policy-relevant solutions capable of contributing to sustainable agrifood systems in the region.

Beyond financial assistance, participants will also gain access to valuable academic resources and learning opportunities.

Access to FAO eLearning Academy

Participants in the competition will benefit from access to the FAO eLearning Academy, FAO’s globally recognized digital learning platform offering UN-certified courses across multiple technical areas related to agriculture, nutrition, food security, sustainability, and rural development.

The eLearning Academy will help participants:

  • Strengthen technical expertise
  • Improve research methodologies
  • Develop evidence-based solutions
  • Understand FAO regional priorities
  • Consolidate research perspectives
  • Enhance academic and professional skills

The platform contains extensive research-based learning materials that can guide participants in refining their research ideas before transforming them into impactful proposals.

Opportunity to Participate in the 10th International Agriculture Congress (IAC 2026)

As part of the initiative, FAO, Universiti Putra Malaysia (UPM), and AUN-AFS will organize an Academia Roundtable during the 10th International Agriculture Congress (IAC 2026), which will be hosted by UPM in Malaysia.

This event will provide finalists with the opportunity to:

  • Engage in regional dialogue on agrifood systems
  • Network with experts, researchers, and policymakers
  • Share innovative ideas and perspectives
  • Develop leadership and communication skills
  • Gain exposure in an international academic environment

The roundtable aims to create a collaborative atmosphere that encourages youth leadership, intellectual exchange, and innovative thinking.

Statements from Organizers and Partners

According to Prof. Dr. Loh Teck Chwen, Dean of the Faculty of Agriculture at Universiti Putra Malaysia, the collaboration between FAO and AUN-AFS creates meaningful opportunities that support:

  • Youth engagement
  • Innovation
  • Food security
  • Agrifood systems transformation

He emphasized the importance of investing in the next generation of researchers and leaders who will help shape a healthier and more sustainable future.

Ms. Kaori Abe, Head of Partnerships at the FAO Partnerships and UN Collaboration Division, also highlighted the importance of collaboration between FAO, universities, academic networks, and students in addressing urgent regional and global challenges.

She stressed that young people across the region possess extraordinary talent, innovation, and commitment, and that collaborative action can transform potential into impactful solutions for people, the planet, and future generations.

Who Can Apply?

The competition is open to:

  • Master’s students
  • Young researchers

Applicants must come from eligible universities within:

  • ASEAN member countries
  • China
  • Japan
  • South Korea

Interested applicants are encouraged to consult the official list of eligible universities before submitting their applications.

Deadline

Applications must be submitted before:

Sunday, 31 May 2026

Late applications may not be considered, so applicants are encouraged to prepare and submit their proposals early.

How to Apply

 

Apply here

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Job opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post Amplifying Young Voices for a Sustainable Future: FAO & AUN-AFS Research Proposal Competition for Youth 2026 Opens Applications appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Ghana TVET Service Recruitment 2026: Apply for Teaching, Administrative, and Support Staff Positions Across Ghana
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The Ghana TVET Service Recruitment 2026 portal is officially open, offering a major employment opportunity for qualified professionals, instructors, administrators, and support staff who want to contribute to technical and vocational education across Ghana. The Ghana TVET Service (GTVET) is responsible for recruiting and posting trained and qualified personnel to public technical and vocational institutions […]

The post Ghana TVET Service Recruitment 2026: Apply for Teaching, Administrative, and Support Staff Positions Across Ghana appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Ghana TVET Service Recruitment 2026 portal is officially open, offering a major employment opportunity for qualified professionals, instructors, administrators, and support staff who want to contribute to technical and vocational education across Ghana.

The Ghana TVET Service (GTVET) is responsible for recruiting and posting trained and qualified personnel to public technical and vocational institutions nationwide. The 2026 recruitment cycle covers all 16 regions of Ghana and includes opportunities in more than 230 accredited TVET institutions.

This nationwide recruitment exercise aims to strengthen Ghana’s technical and vocational education system by hiring competent professionals who can help develop a skilled workforce for the future.

The recruitment process is fully online, transparent, and structured to ensure equal opportunity for qualified applicants.

About the Ghana TVET Service Recruitment Portal

The Ghana TVET Service Recruitment Portal serves as the official platform for all TVET recruitment activities in Ghana.

Through this portal, applicants can:

  • Register online
  • Purchase recruitment vouchers
  • Upload required documents
  • Apply for vacancies
  • Track application progress
  • Receive appointment letters digitally

The Ghana TVET Service has emphasized that all postings, appointments, and transfers are managed exclusively through the official recruitment portal. Applicants are strongly advised to avoid unofficial recruitment channels and scams.

Categories of Vacancies Available

The 2026 recruitment cycle includes opportunities in three major categories:

  1. Teaching Staff

Teaching positions are available for:

  • Tutors
  • Facilitators
  • Senior Tutors
  • Technical Instructors
  • Technical Assistants

Vacancies cover multiple technical and vocational disciplines, including:

  • Engineering
  • ICT
  • Hospitality
  • Fashion
  • Building and Construction
  • Agriculture
  • Technical Education
  • Applied Sciences

These professionals will play a vital role in training students with practical and industry-relevant skills.

  1. Administrative Staff

Administrative positions include:

  • Managers
  • Assistant Managers
  • Senior Assistants

These staff members will oversee institutional administration, operations, policy implementation, and management functions within TVET institutions.

  1. Support Staff

Support roles include:

  • Matrons
  • Assistant Matrons
  • Catering Staff
  • Security Guards
  • Drivers
  • Labourers
  • Cleaners

These positions are essential for maintaining the smooth operation of TVET institutions nationwide.

Nationwide Coverage Across Ghana

The Ghana TVET Service recruitment covers:

  • All 16 regions of Ghana
  • Over 230 accredited TVET institutions
  • More than 50 training programmes

This large-scale recruitment exercise demonstrates Ghana’s commitment to strengthening technical and vocational education nationwide.

Why the Ghana TVET Recruitment Matters

Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) plays a critical role in national development by equipping learners with practical skills required in modern industries and labor markets.

The Ghana TVET Service seeks professionals who can help:

  • Improve technical education delivery
  • Enhance workforce development
  • Promote innovation and entrepreneurship
  • Build industry-relevant competencies
  • Support youth employment initiatives
  • Strengthen institutional administration

Successful applicants will contribute directly to shaping Ghana’s future workforce.

Eligibility Requirements for Applicants

Applicants must meet all stated eligibility requirements before submitting an application.

  1. Ghanaian Citizenship

Applicants must:

  • Be Ghanaian citizens
  • Hold legal authorization to work in Ghana
  • Possess a valid Ghana Card number

The Ghana Card will be used during profile verification.

  1. Educational Qualifications

Requirements vary depending on the position category.

Teaching Class Positions

Applicants for Tutor or Senior Tutor positions must possess:

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in:
    • Technology
    • Engineering
    • Education
  • Professional teaching qualification

Technical Instructor Positions

Applicants must hold:

  • Technician Part I, II, or III Certificate
    OR
  • HND/Tertiary Diploma in Technology or Engineering

Administrative Class Positions

Applicants require:

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in:
    • Education
    • Engineering
    • Management
    • Business Administration
    • Public Administration
    • Social Sciences

Catering Class Positions

Applicants should possess:

  • Bachelor’s degree
  • HND
  • Advanced Certificate in:
    • Catering
    • Hospitality Management
    • Home Economics

Security, Driver, and Labourer Positions

Requirements include:

  • WASSCE/SSSCE or BECE
  • Relevant licenses where applicable
  • Physical fitness requirements
  1. National Service Completion

Applicants for Teaching, Administrative, and Catering positions must possess a valid National Service certificate issued by the National Service Authority.

  1. Professional Teaching Qualification

Applicants for teaching positions must possess recognized education qualifications such as:

  • PGDE (Postgraduate Diploma in Education)
  • Equivalent teaching certification

CBT certification is considered an added advantage for senior teaching roles.

  1. Work Experience Requirements

Experience requirements depend on the position level.

Examples include:

  • Senior Tutors and Matrons: Minimum 1 year experience
  • Senior Assistants and Driver Grade I: Minimum 3 years experience
  • Advanced Security and Driver grades: Up to 6 years experience

Entry-level positions may not require prior experience.

  1. Medical Fitness and Clean Record

Applicants must:

  • Be medically fit
  • Meet physical fitness requirements where applicable
  • Have no false qualification declarations
  • Possess a clean criminal record

False declarations may result in disqualification and possible prosecution.

Step-by-Step Application Process

The recruitment process consists of five phases.

Phase 1: USSD Payment and Voucher Access

Applicants must:

  1. Dial the official USSD shortcode
  2. Pay the application fee
  3. Receive a voucher code via SMS

The voucher remains valid throughout the recruitment cycle.

Phase 2: Profile Creation and Document Upload

Required documents include:

  • Ghana Card
  • Academic certificates
  • Transcripts
  • National Service certificate
  • Professional certificates
  • Passport-size photograph

All documents must be certified true copies.

Phase 3: Application Submission

Applicants will:

  • Browse vacancies by region
  • Upload CV and cover letter
  • Answer job-specific questions
  • Submit applications online

Phase 4: Screening and Verification

The Ghana TVET Service will conduct:

  • Document verification
  • Qualification assessments
  • Background checks
  • Candidate shortlisting

Successful candidates will be contacted via SMS and the portal dashboard.

Phase 5: Posting and Appointment

Successful applicants will:

  • Receive institutional postings
  • Download official appointment letters
  • Report to Regional Directors

Advantages of the Online Recruitment System

The recruitment system offers several benefits:

  • 100% online application process
  • 24/7 portal access
  • Digital appointment letters
  • Easy registration and application tracking
  • Transparent recruitment procedures

Important Notice to Applicants

The Ghana TVET Service has emphasized that:

  • Only shortlisted applicants will be contacted
  • All credentials will be verified with issuing institutions
  • Incomplete applications may not be processed
  • False information may lead to prosecution

Applicants are advised to carefully review all requirements before applying.

Official Application Portal

Interested applicants can access the official recruitment portal here:

Ghana TVET Service Recruitment Portal

 

Apply here

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Job opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post Ghana TVET Service Recruitment 2026: Apply for Teaching, Administrative, and Support Staff Positions Across Ghana appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: United Nations JPO Opportunities 2026: Apply for UNDP Human Rights Analyst in Malawi and IFAD Partnership Analyst in Rome
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The United Nations system continues to provide transformative global career opportunities for young professionals through its prestigious Junior Professional Officer (JPO) Programme. Two remarkable opportunities currently attracting international attention are the UNDP Human Rights Analyst position in Malawi and the IFAD Partnership Analyst (Member State Engagement) position in Rome, Italy. These highly competitive international positions […]

The post United Nations JPO Opportunities 2026: Apply for UNDP Human Rights Analyst in Malawi and IFAD Partnership Analyst in Rome appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations system continues to provide transformative global career opportunities for young professionals through its prestigious Junior Professional Officer (JPO) Programme. Two remarkable opportunities currently attracting international attention are the UNDP Human Rights Analyst position in Malawi and the IFAD Partnership Analyst (Member State Engagement) position in Rome, Italy.

These highly competitive international positions offer young professionals the opportunity to gain hands-on experience within the United Nations system while contributing to global development, human rights, governance, rural transformation, resource mobilization, and sustainable development goals.

The positions are designed for ambitious early-career professionals who aspire to build long-term careers in multilateral organizations and international development.

About the United Nations JPO Programme

The Junior Professional Officer (JPO) Programme is one of the most respected entry pathways into the United Nations and international development institutions.

The programme equips talented young professionals with:

  • International work experience
  • Professional mentorship
  • Technical and leadership training
  • Exposure to multilateral cooperation
  • Opportunities to contribute to the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)
  • Career development support
  • Networking opportunities with global experts and policymakers

JPOs work under the supervision of experienced international professionals and actively contribute to the design, implementation, monitoring, and evaluation of development programmes.

The programme also helps participants strengthen technical expertise, leadership abilities, strategic thinking, and intercultural communication skills.

UNDP Human Rights Analyst JPO Position – Lilongwe, Malawi

The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) is recruiting a Human Rights Analyst under the Junior Professional Officer Programme to support governance, rule of law, and human rights initiatives in Malawi.

Position Details

  • Job Title: Human Rights Analyst
  • Organization: UNDP
  • Duty Station: Lilongwe, Malawi
  • Grade Level: P2
  • Contract Type: FTA International (JPO)
  • Contract Duration: 1 year, renewable subject to performance and agreement
  • Bureau: Regional Bureau for Africa (RBA)
  • Career Stream: Governance / Policy and Programme

About UNDP Malawi’s Governance Programme

UNDP Malawi supports the country’s national development agenda and the Sustainable Development Goals through initiatives focused on:

  • Human rights
  • Governance
  • Rule of law
  • Gender equality
  • Peacebuilding
  • Civic engagement
  • Institutional accountability
  • Democratic participation

The Governance Portfolio aligns with Malawi 2063 and Sustainable Development Goals 5 and 16.

The programme focuses on three major pillars:

Pillar I – Effective, Accountable and Responsive Institutions

This pillar strengthens governance systems, accountability mechanisms, and public institutions.

Pillar II – Civic Engagement and Empowerment

This area promotes citizen participation, inclusion, and democratic engagement.

Pillar III – Oversight, Human Rights and Rule of Law

This pillar supports justice institutions, anti-corruption mechanisms, human rights promotion, and the protection of vulnerable populations.

Focus on Vulnerable Groups and Human Rights

One of the defining aspects of the Human Rights Analyst role is its emphasis on promoting and protecting the rights of vulnerable groups, including:

  • LGBTQI populations
  • Women and girls
  • Children
  • Persons with disabilities
  • Refugees and asylum seekers
  • Persons affected by climate change
  • Persons living with HIV/AIDS
  • Persons with albinism
  • Older persons

UNDP works closely with the Malawi Human Rights Commission and other institutions to strengthen inclusive governance and equal access to justice.

Key Responsibilities of the UNDP Human Rights Analyst

The selected candidate will support:

Human Rights and Rule of Law Programming

Responsibilities include:

  • Supporting treaty body reporting mechanisms
  • Monitoring human rights violations
  • Supporting justice and security institutions
  • Coordinating human rights programming
  • Preparing project documents and work plans
  • Monitoring programme implementation
  • Supporting donor reporting

Strategic Partnerships and Resource Mobilization

The JPO will:

  • Engage with civil society organizations
  • Coordinate with development partners
  • Support policy dialogue
  • Organize workshops and consultations
  • Identify opportunities for programme expansion

Knowledge Management and Research

Additional duties include:

  • Producing analytical briefs
  • Supporting monitoring and evaluation
  • Documenting lessons learned
  • Researching international best practices

Qualifications Required for the UNDP Position

Applicants must possess:

Education

  • Master’s degree or equivalent in:
    • Law
    • International Human Rights
    • Development Studies
    • Social Sciences
    • Related disciplines

Experience

  • Minimum 2 years of relevant professional experience
  • Experience with human rights institutions or government institutions
  • Experience in programme planning and reporting
  • Strong Microsoft Office skills

Desired Skills

  • Experience in international human rights systems
  • Experience promoting rights of vulnerable groups
  • Strong analytical and communication abilities

Language Requirement

  • Fluency in English is mandatory

Learning and Career Development Opportunities at UNDP

The selected JPO will benefit from:

  • UNDP induction programmes
